data.title: Constellation Energy raised to Buy at Citi, which says it's time to buy the dip
data.description:
Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) is upgraded to Buy from Hold with a $232 price target at Citi, saying the stock's risk/reward profile looks more attractive after the recent selloff, which saw shares chopped in half from January 24 to April 4.

Citi analyst Ryan Levine highlights several factors contributing to his upgrade, including the potential for co-location deals estimated to involve ~1 GW of uprates and 1.2 GW from existing operations, potential natural gas builds in Texas, a favorable stock issuance by Calpine, and downward protection from power price movements provided by production tax credits.

Levine said he believes Constellation (NASDAQ:CEG) can achieve the $232 PT without any behind-the-meter deals or unannounced growth, and any material change to AI power market policies would create material upside for the company.

Constellation Energy (CEG) shares, which had been trading around the flatline, +16.6% after President Trump announced a 90-day pause on tariffs for most of the world.

In the defensive regulated utility space, Levine reiterates his Buy rating for Consolidated Edison (ED), believing it is uniquely positioned because of a nuance in its earnings outlook, where 93% of 2026 earnings is tied to a financial ROE formula scheduled to be filed in May 2025 that is based on trailing three-month stock prices.

The weaker the peer stocks and the higher the market risk premium, the higher the expected earnings outlook for ConEd (ED), Levine says.

data.title: Pharma, MedTech stocks bounce back as Trump pauses tariffs
data.description:
Global drugmakers and MedTech companies bounced back after U.S. President Donald Trump issued a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for all countries except China, ending a key overhang for the healthcare sector.

While Pharma was immune from Trump’s sweeping tariff measures, which included MedTech last week, Wall Street was bracing for imminent impact given his repeated threats to subject pharmaceuticals to higher tariffs in an attempt to boost domestic production.

Global pharma stocks came under renewed selling pressure in the morning hours Wednesday after Trump threatened to impose a "major" tariff on all pharmaceutical imports “very shortly.”  “We’re going to tariff our pharmaceuticals, and once we do that, they are going to come rushing back into our country, because we’re the big market," he said at a dinner in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday.

With his tariff measures on hold for now, major drugmakers reversed early losses.

Notable outperformers include Viatris  (VTRS), Bausch Health (BHC), Teva Pharmaceutical (TEVA), Novo Nordisk (NVO), Gilead (GILD), Amgen (AMGN), Pfizer (PFE), Eli Lilly (LLY), Merck (MRK) and Vertex Pharma (VRTS). Veterinary drugmakers Elanco Animal Health (ELAN), and Zoetis (ZTS) were also among notable gainers.

VanEck Vectors Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), which represents 25 global pharma stocks, and SPDR S&P Pharmaceuticals ETF (NYSEARCA:XPH) ended a four-day selloff, as did Health Care SPDR ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV).

Meanwhile, iShares U.S. Medical Devices ETF (NYSEARCA:IHI) climbed ~5%, marking its best one-day since November 2022.

Notable gainers in the MedTech: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG), DexCom (DXCM), Baxter International (BAX), ResMed (RMD), Teleflex (TFX), Stryker (SYK), Boston Scientific (BSX), Edwards Lifesciences (EW), TransMedics (TMDX), Becton, Dickinson (BDX), Medtronic (MDT), Glaukos (GKOS).

data.title: WW International sinks on report it's preparing for bankruptcy filing
data.description:
WW International (NASDAQ:WW) plunged 50% on a report that it's preparing for a potential bankruptcy filing in the coming months in a plan that will give control of the business to its creditors.

WW, formerly known as Weight Watchers, is still in discussions with a major group of lenders and bondholders, according to a WSJ report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.  

WW International (NASDAQ:WW) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment. 

Bloomberg reported earlier this month that WW was in talks with its creditors to swap some of its debt for equity  and effectively hand over control of the company to its bondholders.

Shares of WW have plunged more than 75% over the past year as the entry of GLP-1 drugs such as Ozempic has hurt the company's sales.

data.title: Bessent's message to the world: 'do not retaliate, and you will be rewarded'
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent made some quick remarks shortly after President Donald Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for more than 75 countries but a higher levy on Chinese imports.

His message to global trading partners: "do not retaliate, and you will be rewarded."

“We saw the successful negotiating strategy that President Trump implemented a week ago today,” Bessent said. "It has brought more than 75 countries forward to negotiate. It’s a great courage for him to stay the course until this moment, and what we have ended up with here, as I told you, is an escalation.”

This is a developing story. Check back for updates.

data.title: Travel stocks launched higher as recession odds dim
data.description:
Travel and leisure stocks were launched higher in Wednesday’s afternoon trade, riding the wave of optimism that President Trump’s decision to pause U.S. tariffs for 90 days will avert an economic recession.

Airlines and cruise operators are outperforming the rest of the travel sector with gains of 20% or more for names like Royal Caribbean (NYSE:RCL), United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), and Frontier Group Holdings (NASDAQ:ULCC) followed by outsized gains in the leisure category to include Six Flags Entertainment (NYSE:FUN), United Parks and Resorts (NYSE:PRKS), and Disney (NYSE:DIS).

Casino stocks are also ripping higher, with Bally’s Corp (NYSE:BALY) leading the pack with a 35% gain from Tuesday’s close. And despite an exposure to Macau (which remains under a heavy tariff burden) Wynn Resorts (NASDAQ:WYNN) is trading 12% higher, Caesar’s Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) up 12%, and Las Vegas Sands (NYSE:LVS) up by 8%.

Finally, among hotels and resorts, Hyatt Hotels (NYSE:H), Hilton Worldwide (NYSE:HLT), Wyndham Hotels & Resorts (NYSE:WH) and Marriott International (NASDAQ:MAR) are all currently trading with double-digit gains.

data.title: White House steps back from potential export curbs on Nvidia's H20 chips: report
data.description:
The Trump Administration is apparently pausing the implementation of an export ban to China for Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) H20 graphic processing units, according to a report by NPR.

The H20 is the most advanced chip offered by Nvidia that is still allowed to be exported to China. Industry observers and Chinese tech companies had expected the H20 to be added to the list of other banned hardware. Alibaba (BABA), Tencent (OTCPK:TCEHY) (OTCPK:TCTZF) and ByteDance (BDNCE) placed at least $16B in orders for Nvidia's H20 server chips in the first three months of 2025, ahead of a potential U.S. ban on the sale of the AI chip in China.

The H20 chips are slower in performance than Nvidia's new Blackwell chips, which the company sells in most markets outside China. 

Nvidia shares spiked 14% during early afternoon trading on Wednesday. 

The change of heart by the White House came after Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang attended a $1M per head dinner at Mar-a-Lago last week, according to the report by NPR. Nvidia allegedly said it would make new investments in U.S.-based AI data centers.

Nvidia declined to comment on the report.

Also on Wednesday, Trump issued a 90-day pause on most of the new tariffs that just went into effect after midnight. However, China was not exempted. Rather, Trump said he was raising tariffs there even further - to 125%.

"I have authorized a 90 day PAUSE, and a substantially lowered Reciprocal Tariff during this period, of 10%, also effective immediately," he added.

Semiconductor stocks across the board began rebounding on the news, many posting double-digit gains. Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) jumped 12%, Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) and Qorvo (NASDAQ:QRVO) both surged 16% and Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) rocketed 18%. Even Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) soared 17% higher. 

Not one name on Seeking Alpha's Top Semiconductor Stocks list was in the red.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) surged a staggering 15%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Fed policymakers discussed increased risk of inflation at March meeting: FOMC minutes
data.description:
Federal Reserve officials saw an increased risk that tariffs would boost inflation, even though, at that time, they didn't know the magnitude of tariffs that President Donald Trump would impose in early April, according to the minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee's March 18-19 meeting released on Wednesday.

"Several participants also noted that their contacts were already reporting increases in costs, possibly in anticipation of rising tariffs, or that their contacts had indicated willingness to pass on to consumers higher input costs that would arise from potential tariff increases."

Recall that the Fed policymakers kept the benchmark rate unchanged at 4.25%-4.50% at the meeting. All the FOMC's voting members agreed to the decision.

Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: SA Asks: Is gold still a safe haven for investors?
data.description:
Is gold still a safe haven for investors?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Anna Sokolidou and Power Hedge for their thoughts.

Anna Sokolidou: Gold is still a safe haven asset class that also protects your savings from inflation. However, in the short term it depreciates when all asset classes depreciate, while the US dollar rises. In the longer term, as central banks start easing, it surges in value. Unlike fiat currencies, gold is a highly reliable store of value.

Power Hedge: Gold has worked as a store of value for thousands of years. That is not going to change just because of a few days of bad performance in the domestic market. One of the biggest factors that has been driving the gold rally over the past few years has been buying from central banks - particularly in Russia, China, and other developing nations in Eastern Europe and the Southern Hemisphere. These central banks are not sensitive to price. They are buying for policy reasons and do not really care what the price of gold does. 

We can see the effects of this central bank buying by watching the price of gold overnight. As I write this at 7:40 a.m. EST on April 8, the spot price of gold is up 1.84% relative to the close in the American markets last night. It has been like this ever since the tariff-induced market crash started.  

Basically, the price of gold rises overnight due to buying pressure from China, India, Russia and a few other nations, and then the American market pummels it back down with indiscriminate selling. The central bank buying from these nations will not stop, but it is very possible that the American market will stop indiscriminately selling gold and other precious metals. 

Over the long term, gold is still likely to be a safe haven, but this has not rendered it immune to the widespread sell-off in the panic-driven American markets.

data.title: BASF sues Berkshire's Duracell for allegedly stealing battery secrets
data.description:
BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) has filed a lawsuit against battery maker Duracell, owned by Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B), for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to the chemical company's lithium ion battery technology, according to court documents made public in Delaware federal court on Tuesday.

In a heavily redacted complaint, BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) said the trade secrets related to its efforts over several years to manufacture lower-cost materials for high-powered batteries.

BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) said it invested substantial resources in creating a proprietary and trade-secret process for producing high-performance cathode material, and Duracell misappropriated secrets while the companies had a collaboration agreement, shared details with a third party, and arranged for manufacturing under the false pretense that BASF's process came from Duracell.

By "cutting BASF out of its role as developer and supplier," Duracell caused "substantial and irreparable injury," justifying damages, BASF (OTCQX:BASFY) said in the complaint.

Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) bought Duracell from Procter & Gamble in 2016 for ~$2.9B.

data.title: Bally's soars after Star Group accepts a broaded funding deal
data.description:
Bally's Corporation (NYSE:BALY) soared 34.6% on Wednesday afternoon after Star Entertainment Group (OTCPK:EHGRF) finalized a rescue deal with the company to address its own financial struggles and regulatory challenges.

The original agreement, initially valued at A$300 million ($180 million), involved Bally's acquiring a 56.7% controlling stake in Star through convertible notes and subordinated debt instruments. However, in a new development, Investment Holdings Pty Ltd. said it would contribute AU$100 million to the funding package to reduce Bally's (NYSE:BALY) investment to AU$200 million (US$120 million). 

The deal could still see eventually see Bally's (BALY) take control of The Star Entertainment (OTCPK:EHGRF). 

Star Entertainment (OTCPK:EHGRF) was formerly known as Echo Entertainment and has undergone significant transformations over the years. Key properties owned by the company include The Star Sydney, The Star Gold Coast, and Treasury Brisbane. It has been involved in major projects, including the development of Queen's Wharf in Brisbane, in partnership with Hong Kong-based companies Chow Tai Fook Enterprises and Far East Consortium.
